narrative.description: Dark Souls is an action RPG known for deliberate combat, stamina management, and interconnected level design with bonfire checkpoints.
narrative.core_loop: Explore hostile areas, fight enemies and bosses using stamina-limited attacks and dodges, rest at bonfires to heal and respawn, and unlock shortcuts and upgrades.

mechanics.map_notes: In Dark Souls, grip, breath, or stamina meters limit sustained climbing and sprinting.
mechanics.expression: In Dark Souls, grip, breath, or stamina meters limit sustained climbing and sprinting.

mechanics.map_notes: In Dark Souls, the primary offensive loop uses close-range melee strikes rather than projectile weapons.
mechanics.expression: In Dark Souls, the primary offensive loop uses close-range melee strikes rather than projectile weapons.

mechanics.map_notes: In Dark Souls, designated rest points save progress, restore health or resources, and set respawn locations on death.
mechanics.expression: In Dark Souls, designated rest points save progress, restore health or resources, and set respawn locations on death.

mechanics.map_notes: In Dark Souls, a survival mechanic where the player character possesses a finite quantity of health points that decrease when taking damage, with depletion resulting in failure.
mechanics.expression: In Dark Souls, a survival mechanic where the player character possesses a finite quantity of health points that decrease when taking damage, with depletion resulting in failure.

mechanics.map_notes: In Dark Souls, a combat encounter structure in which an enemy signals an incoming attack through a distinct visual or audio cue before the attack resolves.
mechanics.expression: In Dark Souls, a combat encounter structure in which an enemy signals an incoming attack through a distinct visual or audio cue before the attack resolves.

design_intent.player_fantasy: Experience Dark Souls through its core action rpg loop.
